seo.title: Context Window Management for AI Agents in Flowise Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix context window management for ai agents in Flowise agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Flowise, Context Window Management issues for AI Agents manifest as token overflow in long chatflow chains where conversational history accumulates without truncation, causing LLM API errors or incoherent responses. Drag-and-drop RAG pipelines often retrieve excessive vector documents that exceed context limits when combined with chat history. Multi-agent handoffs lose critical context between sequential nodes, while custom tool nodes append unfiltered outputs that bloat the prompt indiscriminately.

howItManifests.symptom: LLM nodes return 'context_length_exceeded' errors after 10+ turns
howItManifests.impact: Chatflow crashes mid-conversation, users must restart

howItManifests.scenario: RAG pipeline with vector store retrieval
howItManifests.symptom: Retriever node returns 15+ documents regardless of relevance
howItManifests.impact: Prompt tokens exceed 128k limit, responses become generic

howItManifests.symptom: Agent2 receives only last message, ignores Agent1 context
howItManifests.impact: Downstream agents make decisions without full conversation state

howItManifests.symptom: Tool outputs (JSON, tables) appended raw without summarization
howItManifests.impact: Context balloons from 4k to 80k tokens after 3 tool calls

detection.name: Token Overflow Monitor
detection.description: Logs token usage per node and flags chains exceeding 80% of model limit

detection.name: Vector Retrieval Bloat Detector
detection.description: Scans retriever nodes for excessive k-value and missing relevance filtering

detection.name: Context Handoff Breakage Scanner
detection.description: Identifies sequential agent flows missing shared memory context propagation

fixes.name: Dynamic Context Truncator Node
fixes.description: Custom Flowise node that truncates history to fit model context window

fixes.name: Smart Retriever with Reranking
fixes.description: Retriever node config with relevance scoring and top-3 filtering

fixes.name: Shared Memory Handoff Wrapper
fixes.description: Global conversation state propagation between agent nodes

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Context Window Management for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates as a callback handler in Flowise chatflow configuration, automatically injecting token monitoring, context truncation, and retrieval optimization across all nodes. When context exceeds 80% of model limits, Vex triggers remediation workflows that rewrite prompts, summarize history, and rerank retrieved documents in real-time without breaking the conversation flow. The SDK captures Flowise's internal node execution traces, identifies the exact failure point (BufferMemory overflow, retriever bloat, or handoff drops), and applies framework-specific patches using Flowise's node templating system.

Vex's agent reliability layer runs parallel to Flowise execution, maintaining a shadow context window that predicts token overflow 2-3 turns ahead. When risks are detected, it injects corrective nodes dynamically via Flowise's API without requiring canvas redesign. This ensures production chatflows remain stable even as conversation length grows or document retrieval scales.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex in 2 lines and eliminate context window failures across all Flowise flows: npm install @vex_dev/sdk
